tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent $94.29 May 27 confirmed (CNBC close, -5%; Rubio 'every chance to succeed'); but White House calls MOU claim 'complete fabrication'. Fresh US strikes + IRGC retaliation (May 27-28) reopen tail risk. Stress (47%) + Tail (28%) = 75% continued disruption pricing. Stop-loss: Brent sub-$85 on confirmed de-escalation + mine clearance. Thesis partially restored on renewed escalation. Conviction raised to medium-high.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent $94.29 May 27 confirmed (-5%); ASEAN Energy ~128.0 est. (-3.4%); ASEAN Banks ~191.0 est. (+2.1%); iTraxx Asia IG ~172bp (-6bp est.). White House 'fabrication' denial + fresh US strikes May 27-28 reopen tail risk - energy long thesis partially restored. Banks rally on cease-fire extension report (May 28). Tail 28%. If deal collapses: energy equities rebound, banks sell off.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short - Hold Pending Deal Outcome
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP ~57.10 est. (-0.5% May 27; PHP strengthening on Rubio deal optimism). White House 'fabrication' denial + fresh strikes reopen tail risk - thesis partially restored. Tail 28% justifies USD Call/PHP Put 61.00 overlay. Nuclear sequencing + Strait sovereignty + renewed military exchanges sustain collapse risk. Stop-loss: confirmed MOU signature + mine clearance (below 57.00). Conviction raised to low-medium.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short - Hold Pending Deal Outcome
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR ~16,600 est. (-0.7% May 27; IDR strengthening on Rubio deal optimism + lower Brent). Brent $94.29 May 27 - ICP overshoot ~$24/bbl easing materially. But White House 'fabrication' denial + fresh US strikes reopen tail risk. Tail 28%: collapse drives IDR weaker on Brent spike. Stop-loss: USD/IDR 16,400 (confirmed Hormuz reopening + P&I).

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia IG CDS Protection - Hold at 178bp
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia IG ~172bp (-6bp May 27 est.); ASEAN HY ~540bp (-10bp est.). White House 'fabrication' denial + fresh US strikes + IRGC retaliation (May 27-28) reopen tail risk - CDS protection thesis partially restored. Tail (28%): deal collapse drives iTraxx 230-260bp; stress (47%): stabilises 165-200bp. Remove at confirmed MOU signature + mine clearance + spreads below 165bp. Not yet at that level.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold on Physical Supply Lag
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM $18.81 est. (Platts May 22); TTF €44.00 est. (-5.8% May 27). White House 'fabrication' denial + fresh strikes (May 27-28) reopen tail - Ras Laffan restart still not before late Aug 2026; mine-clearing ~6 months delays Qatar LNG resumption regardless of deal outcome. JKM-TTF structural premium intact. Stress target $16-20 JKM; tail $24-28. Stop at confirmed mine clearance + Ras Laffan restart timeline.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: iTraxx Asia IG ~178bp (-6bp May 27 est.) - tightening on deal 'largely negotiated'; window widening. Execute any residual private placements now before deal either collapses (tail 23% - spreads spike to 230-260bp) or fully signs (credit demand surge from deal-related issuance). Tail: collapse targets 230-260bp.

Brent $94.29 May 27 confirmed (+0.99%, TradingEconomics); THB ~32.90 est. (-0.9%); Thailand 10Y ~2.18% est. (-4bp); tail 23%. Deal 'largely negotiated' - PTTEP EBITDA moderates at lower Brent; execute USD H2 prefunding immediately. Window closing: if deal signed, credit market tightens sharply; if collapsed (tail 23%), spreads spike. OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) Gulf JV sanctions-compliance unchanged.

iTraxx Asia IG ~178bp (-6bp May 27 est.); USD/SGD ~1.2660 est. (-0.5%); Brent $94.29 May 27 confirmed. Deal 'largely negotiated' - spreads tightening, window opening. Execute revolving-credit extension now: tail (23%) collapse scenario still targets 230bp+; deal signature scenario brings competing issuance. Full USD hedging: SGD strengthening on deal news reduces hedge cost.

ASEAN HY ~550bp (-13bp May 27 est.); Brent $94.29 May 27 confirmed. Deal 'largely negotiated' - spreads tightening, window opening. Hyflux desalination power cost easing on Brent $98 vs prior $104+. Advance private-credit and quasi-sovereign restructuring terms now before deal binary resolves. Tail (23%): collapse pushes ASEAN HY to 620-650bp.
